title: The Case For US Bank Stocks
gist: US bank stocks have rebounded sharply (S&P 500 Banks +13% since June 1 vs a flat S&P 500) as the three fears that drove 15pp of H1 underperformance — a proposed credit-card rate cap, private-credit contagion, and oil-driven rate-hike risk — have all receded, leaving a strengthened fundamental backdrop.

insights.claim: The S&P 500 Banks index has gained 13% since June 1 while the broad S&P 500 has moved sideways, a sharp reversal after banks underperformed the index by 15 percentage points over the first five months of the year.
insights.speaker: Tan Kai Xian
insights.so_what: Momentum has flipped decisively in favor of US bank stocks, marking a potential regime change from H1 underperformance to leadership.
insights.quote: While the S&P 500 index has moved sideways since June 1, the S&P 500 Banks index has gained a robust 13%.
insights.locator: While the S&P 500 index has moved sideways

insights.claim: The fundamental backdrop for US banks has strengthened considerably, making the case for US bank stocks as investor concerns ease.
insights.speaker: Tan Kai Xian
insights.so_what: Signals a constructive stance on US financials, not just a technical bounce — a reason to add exposure to bank equities.
insights.quote: More importantly, the fundamental backdrop for US banks has strengthened considerably.

insights.claim: Trump's January 10 proposal to cap credit card interest rates at 10% for a year unsettled bank investors but ultimately stalled after opposition from Congressional Republicans and the banking industry, removing a policy overhang.
insights.speaker: Tan Kai Xian
insights.so_what: The feared start of an anti-bank policy push has failed, de-risking a key regulatory/political tail for financials.
insights.quote: President Donald Trump’s proposal on January 10 to cap credit card interest rates at 10% for a year unsettled investors

insights.claim: Fears that mounting stress in the US private credit market would spread contagion to commercial banks have been a drag on bank stocks but are now largely behind investors.
insights.speaker: Tan Kai Xian
insights.so_what: Watch private-credit stress as the key reversal risk; its abatement is a core pillar of the bank re-rating.
insights.quote: mounting stress in the US private credit market raised fears of contagion spreading to commercial banks
insights.locator: Second, mounting stress in the US private credit market

insights.claim: The oil price spike following the outbreak of the Iran war raised the risk of US rate hikes, which would have squeezed bank profitability by pushing deposit costs higher — a concern now largely dissipated.
insights.speaker: Tan Kai Xian
insights.so_what: Bank margins are sensitive to deposit-cost pressure from rate hikes; easing oil/rate-hike risk supports profitability.
insights.quote: the spike in oil prices following the outbreak of the Iran war increased the risk of US rate hikes, which would have squeezed bank profitability by pushing deposit costs higher
insights.locator: Third, the spike in oil prices
